response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise, but he does not meet the core requirements for this role. His C++ experience is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets technical experience, and while RIDE Capital involved fintech-adjacent work, it was entirely in Node.js/TypeScript/GraphQL. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, no Summit platform knowledge, and no Python or messaging framework experience. The gap between his actual profile and the mandatory requirements is too wide to bridge.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience across multiple roles, aligning with a desirable skill in the JD
Java background from Altom Consulting and robotic arm work satisfies one optional desirable skill
RIDE Capital role shows some exposure to the investment/fintech domain, even if not capital markets specifically
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months in 2014–2015 at National Instruments), nowhere near the required 5–10 years on Windows
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets technical domain knowledge
No SQL Server or Oracle experience evident; SQL is listed as a skill but without depth or financial-systems context
